source_paper_title: On Averaging and Extrapolation for Gradient Descent
context:
Section 5.1 (Numerical Extensions), page 24, Conjecture 5.1

We expect dual certificates proving a benefit from small extrapolations exist but have not identified their structure and hence leave proving such parallel results open.

Conjecture 5.1. Consider a gradient descent algorithm of fixed length \(N\), constant stepsize \(h \in (0, 1]\), and with \(\|x_0 - x_\star\| = D\). The critical value \(c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}} = 1 + \Theta(1/N)\), ensuring for any \(c \in [1, c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}]\), simple extrapolation by factor \(c\) has a tight worst-case bound of \(\|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\| \le \frac{LD}{Nhc+1}\).
title: Tight worst-case gradient-norm guarantee for simple extrapolation in gradient descent
latex:
Given integers \(N\ge 1\) and dimension \(m\ge 1\), constants \(L>0\), \(D>0\), and a stepsize \(h\in(0,1]\), consider the class \(\mathcal F_{L,D}\) of all pairs \((f,x_0)\) such that:
- \(f:\mathbb R^m\to\mathbb R\) is convex and differentiable and has \(L\)-Lipschitz gradient, i.e., \(\|\nabla f(x)-\nabla f(y)\|\le L\|x-y\|\) for all \(x,y\in\mathbb R^m\);
- \(f\) attains its minimum at some \(x_\star\in\mathbb R^m\);
- \(\|x_0-x_\star\|\le D\).

Run \(N\) iterations of (fixed-stepsize) gradient descent
\[
 x_{k+1}=x_k-\frac{h}{L}\nabla f(x_k),\qquad k=0,1,\dots,N-1.
\]
For an extrapolation factor \(c\ge 1\), form the post-processed output
\[
 x_\sigma = x_0 + c\,(x_N-x_0).
\]
Define the worst-case gradient-norm performance of this extrapolated output by
\[
\mathcal G(N,h,c;L,D):=\sup_{(f,x_0)\in\mathcal F_{L,D}} \|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\|.
\]

Determine the asymptotic size (as a function of \(N\)) of the largest \(c\ge 1\) (call it \(c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}\)) such that the tight worst-case guarantee
\[
\mathcal G(N,h,c;L,D)=\frac{LD}{Nhc+1}
\]
holds for all \(c\in[1,c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}]\). In particular, decide whether \(c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}=1+\Theta(1/N)\) and prove (or refute) the tight bound \(\|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\|\le \frac{LD}{Nhc+1}\) for all \(c\le c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}\).
open_problem_quote:
We expect dual certificates proving a benefit from small extrapolations exist but have not identified their structure and hence leave proving such parallel results open.

Conjecture 5.1. Consider a gradient descent algorithm of fixed length \(N\), constant stepsize \(h \in (0, 1]\), and with \(\|x_0 - x_\star\| = D\). The critical value \(c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}} = 1 + \Theta(1/N)\), ensuring for any \(c \in [1, c_{\mathrm{crit,grad}}]\), simple extrapolation by factor \(c\) has a tight worst-case bound of \(\|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\| \le \frac{LD}{Nhc+1}\).

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
